value: lefthook + beads cannot share core.hooksPath. Discovered while fixing obsidian-mcp-server-2xp. Both tools want to own git's hook path. Symptoms in order: (1) lefthook install refuses to clobber when bd already set core.hooksPath = .beads/hooks → bun install fails in the prepare script; (2) calling 'lefthook run pre-commit' from inside a beads hook script triggers lefthook's auto-sync on every invocation, which then writes its own shim into .beads/hooks/pre-commit and renames the original to pre-commit.old — same conflict, inverted. The ONLY stable arrangement is to never let the lefthook binary execute as part of the git hook flow at all. Fix landed in commit e08738b: inlined oxlint + dprint check directly into .beads/hooks/pre-commit (outside the bd BEGIN/END markers), keeping lefthook.yml only as a config artefact / documentation. Do not try options (a)-(d) from the bd 2xp description — they all eventually re-trigger the auto-sync war.
